senses.gloss: passive participle of أَحَبَّ (ʔaḥabba): loved, beloved

examples.text: وَلَقَدْ نَزَلْتِ فَلَا تَظُنِّي غَيْرَهُ // مِنِّي بِمَنْزِلَةِ المُحَبِّ المُكْرَمِ
examples.english: And you have occupied, have no doubt in it, // in my heart the place of loved and honored ones.

etymology.text:
Derived from the active participle of the verb أَحَبَّ (ʔaḥabba, “to love”).

Derived from the passive participle of the verb أَحَبَّ (ʔaḥabba, “to love”).
